title: Global search returns zero results for terms that clearly exist
problem_reported: A customer reports that searching for 'invoice 2024-11' returns zero results in global search, even though that exact string appears in a document they can open directly. They can find 'invoice' alone, and '2024-11' alone, but not together.
expected_behavior: Global search tokenizes the query, applies AND semantics across tokens, and returns documents that contain all tokens anywhere in their indexed text.
actual_behavior: Multi-token queries that span word-boundary characters like '-' return zero matches, while each single token returns results.
diagnosis_steps: Inspected the query that the search layer sends to Elasticsearch - it is using match_phrase, not match with AND operator.
Checked the indexed representation of a known matching document - the analyzer splits 'invoice 2024-11' into ['invoice', '2024', '11'] (standard analyzer strips the dash).

match_phrase requires exact adjacency of tokens *as indexed*, so 'invoice 2024-11' in the query tokenizes the same way but match_phrase then requires 'invoice','2024','11' adjacent in the doc, which they are - but the phrase is also subject to slop and position offsets that the index was not built for.
resolution: Switched the global search from match_phrase to match with 'operator: and' and 'minimum_should_match: 100%'. This matches the documented product behavior ('all tokens must appear') without assuming adjacency.
confirmation: Re-ran the reproduction query: 'invoice 2024-11' now returns the expected document and 11 others that contain both tokens. Spot-checked 10 prior support tickets about 'search missing results' - 6 of them now return the expected documents. Added an integration test covering hyphenated-token queries.
